metadata.title: Why the same assistant code runs on Discord, Telegram, and iMessage unchanged
metadata.purpose: Each MCP server is a platform adapter that wraps its API in a standard toolset; missing capabilities are simply omitted. The assistant talks to the MCP server, never the platform. Switching platforms means swapping the server, not the assistant.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: MCP protocol, API abstraction, platform differences
metadata.exclusions: why specific tools are missing; per-server implementation; full tool references
metadata.visual_object: A three-lane diagram — platform APIs (left) → MCP servers translating (middle) → unified tools (right), arrows flowing left to right.
metadata.hook: The same assistant calls `reply`, `react`, and `edit_message` on three platforms whose APIs are completely different.

beats.narration_text: Discord exposes `reply/react/edit/fetch_messages`; Telegram omits `fetch_messages` (no history); iMessage has `reply` and history but no reactions. Yet "reply with this" works identically on all three.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Platform APIs are fundamentally different — some have history, some don't — so how does one assistant work on all of them without a rewrite?
The same assistant calls `reply`, `react`, and `edit_message` on three platforms whose APIs are completely different.

Each MCP server is a platform adapter that wraps its API in a standard toolset; missing capabilities are simply omitted. The assistant talks to the MCP server, never the platform. Switching platforms means swapping the server, not the assistant.
beats.visual: A three-lane diagram — platform APIs (left) → MCP servers translating (middle) → unified tools (right), arrows flowing left to right.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: `reply(text="got it")` → Discord `POST /channels/{id}/messages`; Telegram `sendMessage`; iMessage `osascript … send`. Same code, three translations.

Each MCP server is a platform adapter that wraps its API in a standard toolset; missing capabilities are simply omitted. The assistant talks to the MCP server, never the platform. Switching platforms means swapping the server, not the assistant.
